title: Race-lap relative context features using field percentiles and lagged pit pressure
summary: Pit calls depend on relative, not just absolute, state: whether this driver is older on tyres than peers, losing pace relative to same-compound cars, or entering a lap where many rivals are nearing their windows.
rationale: Pit calls depend on relative, not just absolute, state: whether this driver is older on tyres than peers, losing pace relative to same-compound cars, or entering a lap where many rivals are nearing their windows. The current best solutions use some group medians, but not richer within-race-lap percentiles and pressure features.
implementation_hint: For each `Race_Year x LapNumber`, compute same-lap percentiles/z-scores for `TyreLife`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, `LapTime_Delta`, and `Position` overall and within compound. Add counts/shares of rivals with older tyres, rivals already on alternate compounds, and one-lap-lagged pit counts by compound/stint bucket.
expected_effect: Should improve ranking among simultaneous candidates and capture pit-wave behavior without needing a full sequence model.
risk: Same-lap aggregates must exclude the target only if target leakage can enter through labels; keep features purely from contemporaneous covariates and use lagged event counts when involving pit activity.
